features.summary: Morgan Stanley reported Q3 2022 EPS of $1.47 ($1.53 excluding integration expenses) with ROTCE of 14.6% (15.2% excluding integration). Revenues totaled $13 billion driven by strong fixed income trading ($2.2 billion, highest Q3 in over a decade) and robust Wealth Management performance with $65 billion in net new assets despite challenging market conditions. Investment Banking revenues declined to $1.3 billion due to market volatility and deferred client activity. The firm maintained strong capital position with CET1 ratio of 14.8% and returned $2.6 billion through share repurchases.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued market volatility as the Federal Reserve tightens policy to combat inflation, with rates likely reaching mid-4% range. They anticipate advisory and underwriting activity will resume once there's clarity on macroeconomic conditions including inflation, growth, and geopolitics. The firm remains confident in its diversified business model and expects to continue attracting client assets in Wealth Management while maintaining prudent risk management. Management projects generating $1 trillion in new client money every three years, targeting eventual $10 trillion in client assets.
features.risks: Primary risks include continued market volatility impacting Investment Banking and Investment Management fee revenues, potential credit losses in leverage finance and relationship lending portfolios, and deposit outflow pressures as rates rise. The firm faces margin call risks in securities-based lending as asset values decline, though these are viewed as operational rather than credit risks. Integration expenses from E*TRADE and Eaton Vance acquisitions will continue through 2023, and there's uncertainty around the pace and magnitude of Federal Reserve rate hikes.
features.key_drivers: Wealth Management remains the primary growth driver with strong net new asset flows of $65 billion despite market headwinds, supported by diversified channels including workplace relationships and referrals. Fixed income trading benefited significantly from macro volatility and rate movements. Net interest income grew nearly 50% year-over-year to $2 billion due to rising rates, though deposit mix is shifting toward higher-cost products. The firm's technology investments and integrated platform from E*TRADE and Eaton Vance acquisitions continue to drive operational efficiencies and cross-selling opportunities.
